title: Web Fundamentals, Virtualization and Containers Quiz
description: Answer 15 multiple-choice questions covering frontend basics, virtualization, containers, Docker, operating systems, and cloud infrastructure.

questions.prompt: What does HTML stand for?
Which CSS property changes the layout model so children can align in a row or column?
Which array method creates a new array by transforming every item in the original array?
Which attribute connects a visible label to a form input in standard HTML?
What should happen when a user runs out of time for a question in this quiz?
options: The score should stay the same and the quiz should stop
The question should be marked skipped and the score should decrease by 1
The quiz should restart from the first question
The user should be forced to answer anyway

questions.prompt: What is a container in computing?
options: A package that includes an application and its dependencies

questions.prompt: What happens if the host machine crashes?
Why do applications compiled for Windows often fail to run on Linux without modification?
What is a host OS in Type 2 virtualization?
options: The OS installed inside the VM
The OS on which the hypervisor runs

questions.prompt: What is the main cause of cross-platform execution issues?
options: Differences in operating systems and hardware architectures

questions.prompt: Which cloud computing service model heavily relies on virtualization?
Which statement best describes containers compared to virtual machines?
options: Containers require a full guest OS
Containers share the host OS kernel

questions.prompt: Which component allows multiple operating systems to run on a single physical machine?
What is a Docker image?
options: A snapshot of the host OS
A read-only template for creating containers

questions.prompt: What is a Docker container?
options: A running instance of a Docker image

questions.prompt: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for data encryption?
What is the main security benefit of virtualization?
Which protocol operates at the Transport layer?
Which OSI layer ensures compatibility between systems?
Which file defines how a Docker image is built?
Which OSI layer breaks frames into bits?
Which protocol is used for secure email?
Which orchestration tool is commonly used with Docker?
What is Docker Compose used for?
Which OSI layer handles compression?
Which virtualization technique allows multiple OS instances to share the same kernel?
Which command is used to create and start a container?
What is the primary difference between containers and virtual machines?
options: VMs share the host OS kernel
VMs include a full guest OS

questions.prompt: Which OSI layer provides services like email and file transfer?
What is a zero-day vulnerability?
What is the purpose of hashing?
